question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by exploiting the fact that consensusCount is a long that accumulates validatedSignatures.size() across all hash groups, and by injecting enough hash groups each with enough signatures to overflow consensusCount to a negative value, causing the 'if (consensusCount > 0)' check to fail and throw SignatureVerificationException?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by injecting a StreamFileSignature whose getFilename() returns null, causing the SignatureVerificationException message format 'Consensus not reached for file %s' to produce a NullPointerException or misleading log that masks the real error and prevents recovery?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by supplying a Collection<StreamFileSignature> where all signatures are VERIFIED but each has a unique fileHash of exactly 256 bytes, causing HashMultimap to create one bucket per signature, and the outer loop to iterate over all buckets, each with only one signature whose stake is below the 1/3 threshold, so validate() always throws?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getStakeRequiredForConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by supplying a totalStake of 1 with consensusRatio of 1/3, making stakeRequiredForConsensus = ceil(1 * 0.333...) = 1, and then providing only nodes with stake=0, so canReachConsensus(0, 1) always returns false and validate() always throws?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by injecting a Collection<StreamFileSignature> where the node associated with the first signature returns getTotalStake() == Long.MIN_VALUE (negative), causing BigDecimal.valueOf(Long.MIN_VALUE).multiply(consensusRatio) to produce a large negative threshold, making canReachConsensus() always return true for any stake value and corrupting consensus integrity?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by repeatedly calling validate() with a crafted collection that triggers the SignatureVerificationException path, causing the Downloader to log warnings in a tight loop and exhaust disk I/O or log storage, degrading system performance to the point of service unavailability?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by injecting a Collection<StreamFileSignature> where all signatures have status VERIFIED but getFileHashAsHex() returns an empty string '' for all of them, causing all signatures to be grouped under the same empty-string key in signatureHashMap, and then exploiting any downstream logic that mishandles empty-string hash keys?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by supplying a Collection<StreamFileSignature> where the first signature's node.getTotalStake() returns a value that, when multiplied by consensusRatio and rounded with CEILING, overflows BigDecimal scale, causing an ArithmeticException that crashes the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by injecting a Collection<StreamFileSignature> that is an infinite or lazily-evaluated Iterable, causing the for-each loop in validate() to never terminate and hang the importer thread indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by supplying a Collection<StreamFileSignature> where all VERIFIED signatures have the same fileHash but their nodes all report getStake() == Long.MAX_VALUE / 2, causing stake accumulation to overflow on the second addition and produce a negative stake that fails canReachConsensus()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by exploiting the fact that validate() does not validate that all signatures in the collection belong to the same filename, allowing an attacker to mix signatures from different files so that the first signature's filename is used for all error messages, masking the true failure and preventing operators from diagnosing the DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by injecting a Collection<StreamFileSignature> where each signature's node.getStake() returns a different large value, causing the debugStake tracking variable to be updated on every iteration and consuming excessive CPU in comparison operations across millions of signatures?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by supplying a Collection<StreamFileSignature> where all signatures are VERIFIED and share the same fileHash, but the node for each signature returns getStake() == 0 and totalStake == 3, making stakeRequiredForConsensus = ceil(3 * 1/3) = 1, and total stake = 0, so canReachConsensus(0, 1) returns false and validate() throws?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by injecting a Collection<StreamFileSignature> where the first signature's node.getTotalStake() returns a value that causes getStakeRequiredForConsensus() to return BigDecimal.ZERO (e.g., totalStake=1 with a very small consensusRatio rounded down to 0), making canReachConsensus() always return true for any stake including 0, corrupting consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by injecting a Collection<StreamFileSignature> where the collection's iterator throws a RuntimeException on the second call to next(), causing validate() to crash mid-loop and leave the importer in an inconsistent state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by supplying a Collection<StreamFileSignature> where all signatures have status VERIFIED but the fileHash byte array is zero-length (empty), causing getFileHashAsHex() to return an empty string and all signatures to be grouped under the same empty key, potentially bypassing hash-diversity checks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by injecting a Collection<StreamFileSignature> with exactly one VERIFIED signature whose node.getStake() equals stakeRequiredForConsensus - 1 (just below threshold), causing canReachConsensus() to return false and validate() to throw, blocking ingestion of a legitimate file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by exploiting the fact that validate() reads totalStake only from the first signature's node, and by injecting a crafted first signature with an artificially large totalStake (e.g., Long.MAX_VALUE), making stakeRequiredForConsensus impossibly large and ensuring consensus is never reached?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by injecting a Collection<StreamFileSignature> where all signatures are VERIFIED but each signature's node is the same object (same nodeId), causing HashMultimap to group them all under one key but the stake sum to be multiplied by the count of duplicate entries, potentially causing integer overflow?
